desc:
The slope is a bit more steep here than in other parts of the area. To the west,
the ground rises to what appears to be an overlook of some sort. Apart from
that, the only thing breaking the monotony of the plains is a path leading in a
northeasterly direction.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Long, dry shoots of plains grass grows here. Adapted for the harsh, dry climate,
this annual vegetation rises and falls with the seasons. You note that all seems
peaceful here, the only break in the grass being sparse clumps of bushes and a
few rocks. The plains seem to extend as far as the eye can see.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The plains sprout thick wavy grass that reaches waist high to the average human.
The dirt underlying the grass is loose and crumbly. The only life you detect is
the small desert animals that scurry about looking for food. The grass waves to
and fro with the wind, making endless waves where there is no water.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Pushing your way through the dry, ensnaring limbs gets to be a tedious process
as you are constantly stopping to remove thorns, cobwebs, bugs, and twigs from
your hair and clothes. The terrain is not treacherous, but the vine-like
branches make it easy to slip and hurt yourself if not careful.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
Deciding that you have enough water for the moment, you push through the dense
tangles of dead growth, roots, and thorns. This thicket seems to contain nothing
of interest, especially not the hidden treasure you were hoping to find. Taking
a deep breath, you notice that the strange smell detected earlier is now
stronger.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
This must have been the site of a small campfire a long time ago. As you
continue to peer past the masses of dead brush, you spy a strange looking plant.
Smelling the air confirms that this is the source of the odour you detected
earlier. You search the recesses of your mind trying to recall any mention of a
plant of this nature.
name: A Very Dense Thicket

desc:
Maintaining its north-south direction, the path still has no obvious endpoint.
To the north is more open-space, broken only by the Old East Road. In the more
immediate vicinity, you spy some dense thickets to your east, and a small ridge
of earth to your west. The path continues to the south.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
Here the trail runs north-south while passing by some dense thickets. As you
move along, you detect a fragrant odour. As there seems to be no blossoming
flowers in the area, you find it most odd that a sweet scent could be here.
Perhaps it is something unnatural.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
Here the path slopes gradually upward to the west, ending at a small levee. Your
steps quicken and your heart beats a little faster at the sight of the levee.
After all, isn't one designed to hold back a body of water? To the north is the
small ridge noticed earlier, from this angle however, it looks rather odd. There
is something about it that you cannot seem to figure out. To the east the small
path continues.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
Here the path goes two ways. North, past many miles of cheerless plains
eventually crossing the Old East Road, and west continuing further into the
expanses of rolling plains and empty lands. To the north-west is a natural ridge
covered with tall, dry grass. It is not high, just evidence of where the earth
has pushed upwards in rebellion of its constraint.
name: A Dry Path

desc:
The sharp smell of clover and pine mixes sweetly in the air, contrasting sharply
with that of the northern side of the Old East Road. There, poisoned by evil,
the ground festers with foulness and stench. Yet even here amongst the
vegetation you cannot hide, the thin scraggly weeds and thorny scrub offer
little protection. The path continues north and south.
name: Country Path

desc:
Here the path meanders to the east, wandering into a small thatch of forested
hills. Perhaps there, under the cover of nature, you can find protection from
the evilness that permeates this area. Quickening your pace, you head towards
the unknown, knowing that it must be better than here.
name: Country Path

desc:
You are standing in an open area, surrounded by small weeds and dirt. Up ahead a
small dilapidated village of broken wood and stone can be seen. Perhaps this was
the mining village that supported excavation of Turngurth, now it seems long
deserted.
name: Village Entrance

desc:
Standing on broken bits of gravel and concrete, this was once a very sturdy
road, expected to carry no doubt, the heavy loads of ore and metals gutted from
mining Turngurth. All around you, a somber mist lay, the strength of Angmar is
heavy here. Around you lie the battered remains of some houses.
name: Main Street

desc:
You have entered a simple building, intended to house the miners after a long
day of work. It is scarcely more than your length and width. On the floor lies
the remains of a small straw bed, now overgrown with weeds which have sprung
through the wooden floorboards.
name: Sagging Shack

desc:
Here, underneath the cover of the ramshackled town, seems a perfect place to
have an illicit smuggling headquarters. Apparently, you aren't the first to
realise this, around you lie broken crates of wares and black market items. A
large table stands squarely in the middle of this room.
name: Smuggler's Hideout

desc:
Twisting along the graded trail, ahead of you it bends back to the east,
bringing you closer to the forest. Carefully searching the ground, you notice
odd footsteps seeming to disappear into the brush.. or maybe it is just the
scuffled footprints of a few wandering vagrants.
name: Country Path

desc:
Here the path tackles a sharp rise in the land, tacking back and forth like a
ship sailing against the wind. As you near the forest the evilness around you
seems to dissipate, perhaps you can finally find some peace in the forest ahead.
name: Country Path

desc:
The path that you were near seems to have disappeared in the brush and rocks
around you. To the north, the Old East Road runs swiftly and sharply through
this vile land. The forest ahead of you does seem less haunting than the
surrounding area, but only by a little. Deep shadows loom ahead beckoning you to
challenge their domain.
name: Along the Path

desc:
The forest around you certainly is far from healthy. The trees bend and groan,
trying valiantly to stand firm against the evilness surrounding them. Spaced
widely and stunted, you can easily see through them and to the north, where the
source of the evilness lies.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The ground is hard and rocky here, rising and falling like a stormy sea. You
fight to stand afoot, the small rocks threaten to constantly throw you off
balance. Looking around you see evidence of many beasts, at least it isn't
lifeless, something lives here.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The rich smell of nature is stifled here, replacing it, an odor of sweaty skin
and spit. The trees here are heavily scarred with teeth marks and gnashes.
Around you rest the trampled remains of various decaying beasts. You appear to
have stumbled upon the meeting place of a pack of wild animals.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
Here the trees, while never thick, seem to wane out even more. Only a few lumps
of vegetation can be seen. Most of the landscape consists of rocky barren hills,
and stunted brown shrubs. Nothing here appears to be what you would consider
alive, rather.. simply existing.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
Little shade is afforded by these sickly trees. You are actually thankful of
this, the darkness here does not seem comforting or entirely without an evil
presence. You laugh at yourself, aware of it or not, you find yourself hopping
from sunbeam to sunbeam, avoiding the evil weight of the shadows.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
Twigs snap and crackle under your feet making stealthy movement impossible. Any
animal that wasn't alerted to your presence certainly is now. You glance
backwards, the eerie feeling that something is watching you constantly tugs on
your mind. Looking about, bits of matted bloodied fur littered the area, some
very vicious beasts have made their home nearby.
name: Hilly Forest

desc:
The water here flows up from an underground aquifer. Cold and fresh, it is
naturally filtered by the rocks and gravel below the surface. Looking deep in
the water, you can clearly see the bottom, it even looks like a nice place to
take a swim.
name: Pond

desc:
Hmm.. why do you always seem to do that? You always just have to look
everywhere. Well, now your exploring spirit has gotten you lost.. again!
Fighting your way deep in the thicket, you begin the agonizing and tedious
search for a way out.
name: In the Thicket

desc:
Hmm.. why do you always seem to do that? You always just have to look
everywhere. Well, now your exploring spirit has gotten you lost.. again!
Fighting your way deep in the thicket, you begin the agonizing and tedious
search for a way out.
name: In the Thicket

desc:
Hmm.. why do you always seem to do that? You always just have to look
everywhere. Well, now your exploring spirit has gotten you lost.. again!
Fighting your way deep in the thicket, you begin the agonizing and tedious
search for a way out.
name: In the Thicket

desc:
Hmm.. why do you always seem to do that? You always just have to look
everywhere. Well, now your exploring spirit has gotten you lost.. again!
Fighting in the deep thicket, you begin the agonizing and tedious search for a
way out.
name: In the Thicket

desc:
Hmm.. why do you always seem to do that? You always just have to look
everywhere. Well, now your exploring spirit has gotten you lost.. again!
Fighting your way deep in the thicket, you begin the agonizing and tedious
search for a way out.
name: In the Thicket

desc:
You have entered a simple building, intended to house the miners after a long
day of work. It is scarcely more than your length and width. On the floor lies
the remains of a small straw bed, now overgrown with weeds which have sprung
through the wooden floorboards.
name: Sagging Shack

desc:
The sharp smell of urine and dung cuts through the scented boughs of pine around
you. The ground, carpeted by a bed of long thin needles, muffles your footsteps,
and perhaps those of predators as well. The faint clatter of birds and squirrels
returns to this part of the forest; perhaps all is not evil here.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
The land here is wild and pathless, with bushes and stunted trees growing in
dense patches containing wide barren spaces in between. The grass is scanty,
coarse and grey, and the leaves in the thickets are fading and falling. Further
to the north there is a high ridge raising up out of the fields and to the east
the Mitheithel flows southward until it finally joins with the Bruinen and
becomes the Greyflood.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The forest here consists mostly of beeches, the bark of which has an unwholesome
yellow tinge as if the trees were affected by some strange disease. The
undergrowth is sparse, but there is a lot of bracken that crackles even when
nothing seems to disturb it. To the east the forest becomes even thinner and
eventually ends, giving way to a vast barren plain.
name: A Light Forest

desc:
A light forest begins to the west of you, but on all the other sides the
landscape is barren. Desolate plains stretch all the way to the eastern horizon,
but to the north they begin to rise slowly and eventually end at a vast ridge
that stretches from the east to the west. The vegetation is sparse and
colourless and almost no sounds of wildlife can be heard.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
The plains rise rather steeply towards the ridge which crests to the north. The
ground here is rocky with large boulders scattered across the terrain. Not much
grows here, and the little that does is bleak and grey. There is a whistling
sound in the air as the wind finds its way between the boulders.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
The ground falls away towards the plains in the south and the river in the east.
It is quite rocky here and the most of the soil seems to have grown away. South
of you some stunted trees still grow; but here, the largest vegetation are some
bleak bushes with few leaves.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
A sheer cliff to the north makes it impossible to leave the river in that
direction and the western bank looks steep and difficult to ascend. The river
seems to flow straight from here, towards the south, for many miles.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The river lies nested between vast plains to the west and an old abandoned
farmland to the east. The river flows south for many miles will it eventually
merges with the Loudwater and becomes the Greyflood.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
The river flows south towards the vast unpopulated regions of central Eriador.
On the riverbanks some vegetation slowly grows and a few birds fly high above
the ground. The river will continue for many miles before it reaches Tharbad and
after that the vast sea but to follow the river so far does not seem possible.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
To the east the rocky ground slopes steeply into a river that flows from the
north. A large number of bushes and stunted trees grow here, feeding off the
nearby water. In the gaps between the bushes and trees huge boulders can be
seen. Further to the north the land begins to rise where it finally crests
forming a high ridge five or six miles from here.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Lying near the river to the east, this area seems to have been flooded many
times. Some of the debris washed up by the river lie here and there, most of it
being dead trees and bushes. The ground is quite rocky but small stunted trees
and a few bushes manage to grow in the topsoil. Not much evidence of animal life
can be seen here except for the birds flying high above.
name: Vast Plains

desc:
Here the river Mitheithel, or in the tongue of men the Hoarwell, turns to the
west as it continues to flow southward. The riverbanks are steep and rocky and
here and there on the banks some shrubs and bushes have managed grow.
name: The Mitheithel

desc:
A low pile of rocks hinders easy movement towards the north. These piles, made
by some farmer long ago, once served as a fence forming the boundary of the
wheat fields. This area, never cleared is covered with grey rocks and the soil
between them looks dry and unfertile. Some lonely strands of wheat seems to have
spread here from the old wheat field in the north.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Once, long ago, the border of the ancient kingdom of Rhudaur was just south of
here and on the ground the remains of long forgotten skirmishes can still be
found. Despite the presence of the river Mitheithel, some seven miles to the
west, the soil here is very dry. And while mostly only weeds manage to grow
here, bits of wild wheat can also be found. North of here, the land was once
cultivated many years ago, but is now untended.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Between the sharp stones which litter the ground here, some small bushes and a
few stunted trees have taken root. The only other vegetation that inhabit this
area is the tall and bleak grasses. There are not many wild animals around,
though there might be some fishes in the fast flowing river to the west. On the
other side of the river, nine miles north, a tall ridge hinders travel.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
At the bottom of the short steep slope to the west the river Mitheithel flows
towards the south where it will eventually join with other rivers to become the
Greyflood. The ground nearest the river is muddy and very slippery, but
elsewhere the ground is dry and cracked. While some bushes grow close to the
river, the few trees that managed to sprout here died long ago.
name: Riverbank

desc:
The ground dips down towards the river north and west of here. There are some
faint traces of irrigation channels, that in former times were used to lead
water towards the wheat fields east of here. At the edge of the river there are
piles of debris that have washed up over the years.
name: Riverbank

desc:
To the west the Mitheithel can be seen as it flows from its sources in the
Ettenmoors. The soil is more rocky here than it is in the old wheat fields east
of here. Between the rocks bleak bushes and high grass manage to grow. On the
western side of the river vast plains spread out as far as can be seen.
name: Beside the Old Wheat Field

desc:
The ground here slopes gently westward, towards the far off river. This spot
seems to be the southern edge of an old wheat field. To the south, beyond a low
pile of stones, a cast plain takes over and the ground there becomes rocky and
barren.
name: Old Fields

desc:
This is the border of what seems to be an old wheat field. Men once cultivated
this land but it must have been ages ago. The soil seems to be unfertile but
some scattered straws of wheat still manage to grows here and there. Long ago,
someone took all the stones that were ploughed from the field and used them to
build a low stone wall at the southern border of the field. The wall has slowly
collapsed over the years and nowadays it has begun to look more like a heap of
stones.
name: Old Fields

desc:
Wheat and tall green weeds have managed to grow in the shallow, dry topsoil
found here. Once this area was cultivated farmland, but the farmers are long
gone and the only traces that they were once here are some low stone walls used
to divide the fields and the faint traces of irrigation channels.
name: Old Fields

desc:
A large boulder lies in the middle of the field but the other rocks, which are
scattered across the dry soil, are much smaller. A wide river, towards the west,
forms a boundary between these fields and a vast plain which continues for many
miles. A distant road cuts through the terrain, as it traverses east and west,
with the western section obscured by a high ridge line.
name: Old Fields

desc:
This region, bounded by the river Mitheithel to the north and west, has flooded
many times in the past and faint traces of irrigation channels can still be
found upon the ground. The riverbank down to the water is steep and rocky, but
grass and bushes, which are more abundant close to the river than further
inland, provides some footing on the otherwise slippery bank.
name: Riverbank

desc:
These stables were well made when originally constructed and have withstood the
elements better than the main building. Though, time has taken its toll on this
building but someone has recently done some maintenance to keep it standing and
has fixed a section of the roof to allow for some protection in harsh weather.
name: The Inn's Stables

desc:
The water of the river Mitheithel swirls under the Last Bridge's three great
arches. While the big rectangular stone blocks used to construct the bridge have
been worn smooth by the river, they are still in surprisingly good condition.
High above you, close to the top of the middle arch, some birds have been
recently nesting.
name: Under the Last Bridge

desc:
Standing on broken bits of gravel and concrete, this was once a very sturdy
road, expected to carry no doubt, the heavy loads of ore and metals gutted from
mining Turngurth. All around you, a sombre mist lies, the strength of Angmar is
heavy here.
name: Main Street
